content:
1 hour ago

--

In this article, we are going to explore how to build your custom functions on Google Sheets, much like Excel VBA, but easier. The function we are going to build is called PARSE_TIME(), which converts time in text/string format to decimal format (e.g., “15:00” becomes 0.625 or 54000).
1. Go to Google Drive (https://drive.google.com/).
2. Open a new Google Sheets.
3. Go to “Extensions” > “App Script”.
4. Insert a function in the App Script.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

5. Click “Run” and look at the Execution Log.
Ensure that the execution of the code is completed.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Close the App Script and use the function in Google Sheets.
Type the function in the log below.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

The end.
Press enter or click to view image in full size
